LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GUILLERMO REY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT IN 37.3(D)(2)(A) THAT A LANDLORD REQUEST ARBITRATION WHEN THE GROUNDS FOR THE INCREASE IS CC 1954.53(D)(2). PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ASSERTED IN THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Appearances:
Michael McLaughlin, Esq., from Fried & Williams, 917-575-4574, for Plaintiff and opposing party
Joyce Jordan, Esq., Law Office, 415-621-1984, for Defendant and moving party

rgued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT GUILLERMO REY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. There is no requirement in 37.3(d)(2)(A) that a landlord request arbitration when the grounds for the increase is CC 1954.53(d)(2). Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support all causes of action asserted in the complaint. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
